heading: Carbon cycle research
label: 6711 Carbon cycle research

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: To the extent funds are made available for this purpose, the Secretary shall provide a grant to the Consortium for Agricultural Soils Mitigation of Greenhouse Gases, acting through Kansas State University, to develop, analyze, and implement, through the land grant universities described in subsection (b), carbon cycle research at the national, regional, and local levels.
Colorado State University.
Iowa State University.
Kansas State University.
Michigan State University.
Montana State University.
Purdue University.
Ohio State University.
Texas A&M University.
University of Nebraska.

to conduct research to improve the scientific basis of using land management practices to increase soil carbon sequestration, including research on the use of new technologies to increase carbon cycle effectiveness, such as biotechnology and nanotechnology;

children.label: A
children.text: Federal, State, or private entities; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Department of Agriculture;
to develop necessary computer models to predict and assess the carbon cycle;

children.label: A
children.text: voluntary Federal conservation programs;

children.label: B
children.text: private and Federal forests; and

children.label: C
children.text: other land uses;
to develop outreach programs, in coordination with Extension Services, to share information on carbon cycle and agricultural best practices that is useful to agricultural producers; and

children.label: A
children.text: provide, on a near-continual basis, a real-time and comprehensive view of vegetation conditions;

children.label: B
children.text: assess and model agricultural carbon sequestration; and

children.label: C
children.text: develop commercial products.

children.label: A
children.text: the flux of carbon in soils and plants (including trees); and

children.label: B
children.text: the exchange of other greenhouse gases from agriculture.

children.heading: Eligible entities
children.label: 2 Eligible entities
children.text: Research under this subsection may be carried out through the competitive awarding of grants and cooperative agreements to colleges and universities (as defined in ).section 3103 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: developing data addressing carbon losses and gains in soils and plants (including trees) and the exchange of methane and nitrous oxide from agriculture;

children.label: B
children.text: understanding how agricultural and forestry practices affect the sequestration of carbon in soils and plants (including trees) and the exchange of other greenhouse gases, including the effects of new technologies such as biotechnology and nanotechnology;

children.label: C
children.text: developing cost-effective means of measuring and monitoring changes in carbon pools in soils and plants (including trees), including computer models;

children.label: D
children.text: evaluating the linkage between federal conservation programs and carbon sequestration;

children.label: E
children.text: developing methods, including remote sensing, to measure the exchange of carbon and other greenhouse gases sequestered, and to evaluate leakage, performance, and permanence issues; and

children.label: F
children.text: assessing the applicability of the results of research conducted under this subsection for developing methods to account for the impact of agricultural activities (including forestry) on the exchange of greenhouse gases.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriation
children.label: 4 Authorization of appropriation
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as are necessary to carry out this subsection for each of fiscal years 2002 through 2007.

children.label: A
children.text: changes in carbon content and other carbon pools in soils and plants (including trees); and

children.label: B
children.text: the exchange of other greenhouse gases.

children.label: A
children.text: the results of projects under this subsection; and

children.label: B
children.text: the manner in which the methods used in the projects might be applicable to the operations of the farmers, ranchers, private forest landowners, and State agencies.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 3 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as are necessary to carry out this subsection for each of fiscal years 2002 through 2007.

children.heading: Administrative costs
children.label: f Administrative costs
children.text: Not more than 3 percent of the funds made available for this section may be used by the Secretary to pay administrative costs incurred in carrying out this section.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: g Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $15,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2007 through 2012.
